pattern: Once you know the array's minimum and maximum, any value that sits strictly between them automatically has both a smaller and a greater neighbor somewhere in the array.
hints: You don't need to compare every pair of elements -- think about what the two extreme values of the array tell you.
Find the overall minimum and maximum first; any element equal to either of those can never qualify.
Count how many elements are strictly greater than the minimum and strictly less than the maximum -- that count is the answer.

edge_inputs.description: single element, no smaller or greater element exists
two distinct elements, neither has both a smaller and greater partner
all elements identical, none strictly smaller or greater than any other
middle values repeated between distinct min and max
min and max each repeated, only the true middle values qualify
constraint extremes, min and max at the value bounds

stress.description: large random array within full value range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    nums = [rng.randint(-100000, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]

stress.description: many duplicates clustered around a few distinct values
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    pool = [rng.randint(-10, 10) for _ in range(5)]
    nums = [rng.choice(pool) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
